prompt:
We finally split the panel-yield QA module out of the old factory MES monolith into its own repo, but the extraction only carried the source trees — there is no pom.xml here at all, so nothing builds and `mvn test` won't even start. Set up the Maven build from scratch and then finish the two methods that were still TODO when the code moved.

Build first: write a pom.xml (jar packaging, coordinates com.brightforge:ledyield:0.1.0, UTF-8 source encoding). Our platform team is strict about reproducible builds, so pin EXACT plugin and dependency versions — no version-less plugins riding super-POM defaults: junit-jupiter 5.10.2 (the aggregate org.junit.jupiter:junit-jupiter artifact, test scope — the test class uses @ParameterizedTest/@CsvSource, so params must be on the test classpath), maven-surefire-plugin 3.2.5, maven-compiler-plugin 3.15.0 configured for release 21, maven-resources-plugin 3.4.0.

Then the code, in src/main/java/com/brightforge/ledyield/PanelYieldStats.java (PanelLot is done — leave it alone; its validation messages are already under test):
- classify(PanelLot): SHIP when the lit-pixel rate is 90% or better, REWORK when 70% or better, SCRAP below — boundaries inclusive on the way up (exactly 90.0 is SHIP, exactly 70.0 is REWORK).
- summarize(List<PanelLot>): one line per lot, sorted by lot code, formatted exactly '<lotCode> <model> <rate>% <verdict>' with the rate at one decimal place, e.g. 'RUN-A12 atlas 95.0% SHIP'. Format with Locale.ROOT so the QA board renders the same on every workstation — a line terminal in Lyon once printed '95,0%' and broke the parser downstream. Empty input gives an empty list.

Don't modify src/test/java/com/brightforge/ledyield/PanelYieldStatsTest.java. Done when `mvn -q test` passes from the repo root; the first run may pull the pinned artifacts from Maven Central, which is expected.
